articles.headline: Government Unveils 'National Urban Development Strategy 2047' to Drive Sustainable Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive strategy aiming for planned and sustainable urbanization across India by 2047.
• Key pillars include smart infrastructure development, green urban spaces, affordable housing, and digital governance.
• The strategy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach involving central, state, and local bodies, along with private sector participation.
• Focus areas include enhancing urban mobility, improving waste management, and ensuring climate-resilient urban planning.
• This initiative is envisioned as a critical component of India's 'Viksit Bharat @2047' roadmap.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
Target year for strategy implementation is 2047.
Aims to integrate various urban development schemes under one umbrella.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges of rapid urbanization in India and how this strategy aims to address them.
Evaluate the potential impact of a 'Viksit Bharat @2047' focused urban strategy on economic growth and social equity.
Analyze the role of technology and public-private partnerships in achieving the goals of sustainable urban development.
articles.imageDescription: Blueprint of a futuristic smart city with green spaces and efficient transport systems.
articles.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making sustainable urban planning crucial.

articles.headline: Clashes Erupt in Kokrajhar, Assam, Following Youth's Murder; Two Killed
articles.explainer:
• Violent clashes broke out in Kokrajhar district, Assam, leading to the death of at least two individuals and several injuries.
• The unrest was triggered by the murder of a local youth, escalating into ethnic tensions in some areas.
• Authorities have imposed Section 144 of CrPC and deployed additional security forces to restore order.
• The Chief Minister has appealed for peace and announced a high-level inquiry into the incident.
• This incident highlights the persistent challenges of ethnic harmony and internal security in certain regions of Assam.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
prelimsPoints: Kokrajhar district is located in Assam.
Section 144 of CrPC prohibits assembly of more than four people.
Assam has a history of ethnic and communal tensions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to ethnic clashes in regions like Kokrajhar and suggest long-term solutions.
Discuss the role of state and non-state actors in aggravating internal security challenges in the North-Eastern states.
Examine the effectiveness of administrative measures like Section 144 in managing law and order situations during civil unrest.
articles.imageDescription: Security personnel patrolling a tense street in Kokrajhar, Assam.
articles.trivia: Kokrajhar is part of the Bodoland Territorial Region (BTR), an autonomous administrative unit in Assam.

articles.headline: Nipah Virus Outbreak Reported in West Bengal: Health Alert Issued
articles.explainer:
• An outbreak of the Nipah virus has been confirmed in West Bengal, with at least two cases reported in Barasat, North 24 Parganas district.
• Health authorities have initiated contact tracing, surveillance measures, and established isolation facilities.
• The state government has issued a high alert, advising public on preventive measures and symptoms.
• Nipah virus is a zoonotic disease transmitted from animals (primarily fruit bats) to humans, or through contaminated food, or directly from person-to-person.
• This outbreak underscores the continuous threat of emerging infectious diseases and the need for robust public health infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Nipah virus is caused by a paramyxovirus.
Fruit bats (Pteropus genus) are natural hosts.
Symptoms include fever, headache, drowsiness, disorientation, and mental confusion.
Kerala has previously experienced Nipah outbreaks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in managing zoonotic disease outbreaks like Nipah in a densely populated country like India.
Evaluate the preparedness of India's public health infrastructure to handle emerging infectious diseases.
Explain the 'One Health' approach and its significance in preventing and controlling zoonotic pathogens.
articles.imageDescription: A medical professional in PPE conducting tests in a makeshift laboratory.
articles.trivia: The Nipah virus was first identified in 1998 during an outbreak among pig farmers in Malaysia.

articles.headline: Indian Air Force Pipistrel Virus Aircraft Crashes in Prayagraj; No Casualties
articles.explainer:
• An Indian Air Force (IAF) Pipistrel Virus SW 80 aircraft experienced an engine failure and crashed into a pond near Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh.
• Both pilots safely ejected before the crash, and no injuries were reported.
• A Court of Inquiry has been ordered to investigate the exact cause of the engine malfunction.
• The Pipistrel Virus SW 80 is a light trainer aircraft used by the IAF for basic flying training.
• This incident highlights the importance of regular maintenance and safety protocols in military aviation.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Pipistrel Virus SW 80 is an IAF trainer aircraft.
The incident occurred in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh.
No injuries were reported.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services personnel inspecting the site of a small aircraft crash.
articles.trivia: The Pipistrel Virus SW 80 is manufactured by a Slovenian company, Pipistrel, and is known for its fuel efficiency.

articles.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Tech, Defence, and Green Energy
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral ties between the two nations.
• Discussions focused on deepening cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, defence industrial collaboration, and renewable energy.
• Both sides emphasized expanding trade relations and enhancing coordination on global issues, including climate change and regional security.
• The dialogue also addressed challenges posed by geopolitical shifts and committed to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• This high-level engagement signifies the continued strengthening of the India-US comprehensive global strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Strategic Dialogue is an annual event.
Key areas of cooperation include defence, technology, and clean energy.
Both nations are committed to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India-US strategic partnership and its significance for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss how cooperation in critical and emerging technologies can bolster India's economic growth and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the role of India and the US in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
articles.imageDescription: Diplomats from India and the US shaking hands after signing agreements.
articles.trivia: The US is one of India's largest trading partners and a key source of Foreign Direct Investment (FDI).

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed petitions challenging the Act on grounds of privacy infringement and potential misuse of state power.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• This landmark judgment provides legal clarity and strengthens India's framework for data governance in the digital age.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data, recognizing the right of individuals to protect their personal data.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of IT in governance.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act 2023 was enacted to protect personal data.
Supreme Court upheld its key provisions.
The Act balances individual privacy with state interests.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the balance between individual privacy and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the significance of the DPDP Act 2023 in shaping India's digital economy and protecting citizen's rights.
Critically evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a diverse and digitally evolving nation.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books in front of the Supreme Court of India emblem.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Key Milestones, Boosts Renewable Energy Sector
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission reported significant progress in its initial phase, with several pilot projects achieving operational status.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Key achievements include advancements in electrolyser manufacturing capabilities and development of green hydrogen production facilities.
• Government incentives and partnerships with private sector players are driving innovation and investment in the sector.
• This initiative is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization efforts, and achieving its climate targets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for India to be a global hub for green hydrogen.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy through electrolysis.
It plays a crucial role in India's climate targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and its commitment to climate action.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential of India becoming a global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a hydrogen molecule with green leaves, symbolizing green energy.
articles.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030 under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Inclusive Global AI Governance Framework at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its call for a comprehensive and inclusive global framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance at a United Nations forum.
• Emphasizing ethical considerations, data privacy, and equitable access, India highlighted the need for AI development to benefit all nations.
• The Indian delegation stressed the importance of preventing AI misuse and bridging the digital divide between developed and developing countries.
• This stance aligns with India's domestic efforts to regulate AI while fostering innovation through initiatives like the National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence.
• India's growing technological prowess positions it as a key voice in shaping international norms for responsible AI.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India advocates for an inclusive global AI governance framework.
UN forum is the platform for discussion.
Ethical AI and data privacy are key concerns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in shaping global AI governance norms.
Analyze the ethical dilemmas associated with advanced AI and the need for international cooperation to address them.
Examine how India's domestic AI strategy aligns with its vision for a globally responsible AI ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic representing global connectivity and artificial intelligence, with India's flag subtly integrated.
articles.trivia: India is home to one of the largest pools of AI professionals and researchers globally.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns and Growth Outlook
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• This decision comes amidst persistent inflationary pressures, albeit with signs of moderation, and a stable growth outlook for the Indian economy.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a 'wait and watch' approach, closely monitoring global economic uncertainties and domestic demand.
• The status quo decision impacts lending rates and the overall liquidity in the financial system.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC sets the repo rate.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting is a primary objective of MPC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Analyze the factors influencing the Monetary Policy Committee's decision-making process in the current economic scenario.
Evaluate the impact of a 'status quo' monetary policy on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending and investment.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo with a graph showing economic trends.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members (three from RBI, three appointed by the government).

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase-II Accelerates, Focus on Tributaries and Sustainable River Basin Management
articles.explainer:
• The second phase of the Namami Gange Mission has shown accelerated progress, with increased focus on cleaning tributaries and adopting a holistic river basin management approach.
• New projects initiated include decentralized wastewater treatment plants, solid waste management in towns along tributaries, and ecological restoration.
• The mission emphasizes community participation and leveraging technology for real-time monitoring of water quality.
• This renewed push aims to achieve the 'Aviral and Nirmal Ganga' (uninterrupted and clean Ganga) vision, extending its impact beyond the main river.
• The success of the mission is crucial for public health, biodiversity, and the cultural heritage associated with the Ganga river system.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Mission aims to clean and rejuvenate the Ganga river.
Phase-II focuses on tributaries and holistic basin management.
Ministry of Jal Shakti is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Assess the challenges faced by large-scale river rejuvenation projects like Namami Gange in India and the strategies adopted to overcome them.
Discuss the socio-economic and ecological benefits of a clean Ganga river system for the states it traverses.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological interventions in ensuring the long-term sustainability of river cleaning initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A drone shot of a clean stretch of the Ganga river with new infrastructure visible along its banks.
articles.trivia: Ganga is the longest river of India, flowing for over 2,500 km, and is considered sacred by millions.

articles.headline: India Inaugurates Its First National Quantum Computing Hub in Bengaluru
articles.explainer:
• India officially inaugurated its first National Quantum Computing Hub (NQCH) in Bengaluru, marking a significant leap in advanced technology.
• The hub will serve as a central facility for research, development, and innovation in quantum technologies, including quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
• It aims to foster collaboration between academia, industry, and government to build a robust quantum ecosystem.
• This initiative is part of the National Quantum Mission, which seeks to position India among the leading nations in quantum science and technology.
• Quantum computing holds immense potential for breakthroughs in medicine, materials science, artificial intelligence, and cybersecurity.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: India's first NQCH is in Bengaluru.
Part of the National Quantum Mission.
Focuses on quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of quantum computing for India's national security and economic competitiveness.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in developing a indigenous quantum technology ecosystem in India.
Examine the potential applications of quantum computing across various sectors and its transformative impact.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of quantum bits (qubits) and a microchip, symbolizing advanced computing.
articles.trivia: A 'qubit' (quantum bit) is the basic unit of information in quantum computing, analogous to a bit in classical computing.

articles.headline: India-GCC Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stage
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) have reportedly entered their final stages.
• The agreement aims to boost bilateral trade, investment, and economic cooperation between India and the six GCC member states.
• Key areas of discussion include tariff reduction, market access for goods and services, and investment protection.
• A successful FTA would unlock significant economic opportunities for India, particularly in energy, food security, and remittances.
• This move underscores India's 'Look West' policy and its strategic importance to the Gulf region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: GCC comprises six member states: Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE.
FTA aims to boost trade and investment.
India has strong historical ties with the Gulf region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the GCC countries.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India's economy if the FTA is successfully implemented.
Evaluate how India's 'Look West' policy complements its broader foreign policy objectives, particularly in the context of energy security and diaspora engagement.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states arranged side by side, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: The GCC is a political and economic union of Arab states bordering the Persian Gulf.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Promote Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched a new scheme aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture across vulnerable regions of India.
• The scheme focuses on developing and disseminating stress-tolerant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It integrates traditional knowledge with modern scientific approaches to enhance farmers' adaptability to changing climatic conditions.
• Financial incentives, training programs, and capacity building initiatives are central to the scheme's implementation.
• This move is critical for ensuring food security, increasing farmers' income, and achieving sustainable agricultural development in the face of climate change.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
keyTerms: National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change (NAFCC)
prelimsPoints: New scheme by Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare.
Aims to promote climate-resilient agriculture.
Focus on stress-tolerant crops and efficient water management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by Indian agriculture due to climate change and the need for climate-resilient practices.
Evaluate the potential impact of government schemes promoting climate-smart agriculture on farmers' livelihoods and national food security.
Analyze how traditional agricultural knowledge can be integrated with modern science to build a more resilient farming system.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with modern irrigation techniques and drought-resistant crops.
articles.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture, with millions of farmers dependent on monsoon rains.

articles.headline: New Archaeological Discovery Sheds Light on Indus Valley Civilization's Trade Networks
articles.explainer:
• Archaeologists in Gujarat announced the discovery of new artifacts and structural remains, providing fresh insights into the trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The findings include unique pottery, seals, and remnants of a dock-like structure, suggesting extensive maritime trade with West Asia.
• This discovery challenges some existing theories about the geographical extent and economic activities of the Harappan civilization.
• The ongoing excavation aims to uncover more details about the daily life, social structure, and technological advancements of the ancient inhabitants.
• Such discoveries are crucial for reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding its contributions to global trade routes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Ancient History
prelimsPoints: New discovery in Gujarat related to Indus Valley Civilization.
Findings suggest maritime trade with West Asia.
Lothal and Dholavira are other important IVC sites in Gujarat.
mainsPoints: Discuss how recent archaeological discoveries have enhanced our understanding of the urban planning and trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the significance of maritime trade for the prosperity and cultural exchange of ancient Indian civilizations.
Examine the methodologies employed by archaeologists to reconstruct the socio-economic life of ancient societies.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists meticulously excavating a site, revealing ancient pottery fragments.
articles.trivia: Lothal, an ancient city of the Indus Valley Civilization in Gujarat, is believed to have had one of the world's earliest known docks.

articles.headline: First Vande Bharat Sleeper Train Prototype Unveiled, Set for Trials
articles.explainer:
• Indian Railways unveiled the prototype of its first Vande Bharat Sleeper train, designed to offer premium overnight travel experience.
• The new train set features enhanced amenities, including improved berths, modular interiors, and advanced safety systems.
• This development is part of the broader modernization plan for Indian Railways, aiming to replace conventional sleeper coaches on key routes.
• The Vande Bharat Sleeper is expected to undergo extensive trials before commercial rollout, promising faster and more comfortable long-distance journeys.
• This initiative is a significant step towards 'Make in India' in the railway sector and upgrading passenger experience.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: First Vande Bharat Sleeper prototype unveiled.
Aims to replace conventional sleeper coaches.
Part of Indian Railways' modernization plan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of railway modernization, exemplified by Vande Bharat trains, in enhancing India's connectivity and economic growth.
Analyze the 'Make in India' initiative's impact on the indigenous manufacturing capabilities of the Indian railway sector.
Evaluate how improved passenger experience and faster travel can contribute to regional development and tourism.
articles.imageDescription: A sleek, modern Vande Bharat Sleeper train prototype standing at a railway platform.
articles.trivia: The first Vande Bharat Express (trainset 18) was flagged off in February 2019.

articles.headline: Government Releases Gender Budgeting Report 2026, Highlights Progress in Women-Centric Schemes
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance released its annual Gender Budgeting Statement for 2026, detailing allocations and expenditures on programs benefiting women.
• The report highlighted increased outlays for schemes related to women's health, education, skill development, and safety.
• It also assessed the impact of various government initiatives on gender equality and women's empowerment.
• Gender budgeting is a tool to analyze the government budget from a gender perspective to ensure that policies and programs address gender gaps.
• This continuous focus on gender-responsive budgeting is vital for achieving inclusive growth and fulfilling Sustainable Development Goals.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Finance releases Gender Budgeting Statement annually.
It analyzes government budget from a gender perspective.
Aims to address gender gaps and promote women's welfare.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of gender budgeting and its significance in promoting gender equality and women's empowerment in India.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes designed for women's welfare, as reflected in the Gender Budgeting Report.
Discuss the challenges in implementing gender-responsive budgeting and suggest measures to enhance its impact.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing a balance scale with 'Gender Equality' on one side and 'Budget Allocation' on the other.
articles.trivia: India introduced Gender Budgeting in 2005-06, becoming one of the first countries in the world to adopt this practice.

articles.headline: Ecologists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• A team of ecologists announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats of India.
• The discovery underscores the immense ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot.
• This new species, yet to be formally named, was found in a previously unexplored microhabitat, indicating vast unexplored biodiversity.
• The finding calls for intensified conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the region from habitat loss and climate change.
• Such discoveries contribute significantly to our understanding of India's natural heritage and the global biodiversity inventory.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: New amphibian species discovered in Western Ghats.
Western Ghats is a recognized biodiversity hotspot.
Discovery highlights need for conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the implications of discovering new species for conservation strategies and understanding ecosystem health.
Examine the role of scientific research and community involvement in protecting India's rich biodiversity.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered amphibian, showcasing its unique features.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Sees Boost with New Initiatives and Digital Push
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism announced new initiatives to further boost India's tourism sector, focusing on sustainable tourism and cultural heritage.
• These include enhanced digital platforms for tourist information, promotion of lesser-known destinations, and improved connectivity.
• The initiatives aim to attract both domestic and international tourists, contributing significantly to economic recovery and employment generation.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing niche tourism segments like eco-tourism, adventure tourism, and medical tourism.
• A robust tourism sector is crucial for showcasing India's soft power and cultural diversity on the global stage.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism leads initiatives.
Focus on digital platforms and lesser-known destinations.
Aims to boost domestic and international tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional impact of a thriving tourism sector on India's economy, employment, and soft power.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's tourism industry and how recent government initiatives aim to address them.
Evaluate the potential of niche tourism segments like eco-tourism and medical tourism in promoting sustainable and inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments and natural landscapes.
articles.trivia: The 'Incredible India' campaign is a major initiative by the Ministry of Tourism to promote India as a tourist destination.

highlights: Government unveils 'National Urban Development Strategy 2047' to drive sustainable urbanization and smart city expansion.
Nipah virus outbreak reported in West Bengal, prompting a high health alert and immediate containment measures.
Supreme Court upholds key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, strengthening India's data governance framework.
India-US Strategic Dialogue concludes with enhanced cooperation pledged in critical technologies, defence, and green energy.
India's National Green Hydrogen Mission achieves key milestones, positioning the nation as a potential global hub for green energy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'National Urban Development Strategy 2047':
1. It is launched by the Ministry of Rural Development.
2. Its primary goal is to achieve sustainable urbanization by 2047.
3. It emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach including private sector participation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The National Urban Development Strategy 2047 is launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, not the Ministry of Rural Development. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, as the strategy aims for sustainable urbanization by 2047 and emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach including private sector.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Nipah Virus, consider the following statements:
1. It is a bacterial disease transmitted primarily through contaminated water.
2. Fruit bats (Pteropus genus) are considered its natural hosts.
3. Kerala has previously reported outbreaks of this virus.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Nipah virus is a viral disease, not bacterial, and is transmitted from animals to humans, or through contaminated food, or directly from person-to-person, not primarily through contaminated water. Statements 2 and 3 are correct; fruit bats are natural hosts, and Kerala has experienced previous outbreaks.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is entrusted with the task of setting the policy repo rate and other monetary policy decisions in India to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
2. The Supreme Court recently struck down all its key provisions.
3. It balances individual privacy rights with legitimate state interests.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as the DPDP Act aims to regulate digital personal data. Statement 2 is incorrect; the Supreme Court upheld, not struck down, key provisions of the Act. Statement 3 is correct, as the Act seeks to balance individual privacy with legitimate state interests.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the National Quantum Computing Hub (NQCH) is/are correct?
1. It is India's first such hub, inaugurated in Bengaluru.
2. Its primary focus is on quantum communication only.
3. It is part of the National Quantum Mission.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, the NQCH is India's first and is in Bengaluru. Statement 2 is incorrect; its focus is broader, including quantum computing, communication, and sensing. Statement 3 is correct, as it is part of the National Quantum Mission.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the challenges of rapid urbanization in India and discuss how a comprehensive strategy like the 'National Urban Development Strategy 2047' can address these issues while ensuring sustainability and inclusivity.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce rapid urbanization challenges (housing, infrastructure, environment, social equity). Explain NUD Strategy 2047's objectives (smart infrastructure, green spaces, affordable housing, digital governance, climate resilience). Analyze its potential to address challenges, focusing on sustainability, inclusivity, multi-stakeholder approach. Conclude with implementation challenges and way forward.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Nipah virus outbreak in West Bengal highlights India's vulnerability to emerging infectious diseases. Discuss the 'One Health' approach and its significance in preventing and controlling zoonotic disease outbreaks in a densely populated country like India. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly mention Nipah outbreak. Define 'One Health' approach (interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health). Explain its significance for zoonotic diseases in India (early detection, coordinated response, surveillance, public awareness, inter-sectoral collaboration). Discuss challenges and way forward for effective implementation.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for global AI governance and its domestic strategy for artificial intelligence reflects a nuanced approach to technological advancement. Discuss India's vision for responsible AI development and its role in shaping international norms for ethical AI.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's increasing role in global tech. Elaborate on India's vision for responsible AI (ethical considerations, data privacy, equitable access, preventing misuse, bridging digital divide). Connect this to India's domestic National Strategy for AI. Discuss India's efforts at UN forums to shape international norms. Conclude with the importance of India's voice as a major tech consumer and developer.

quickBytes.text: The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'National Urban Development Strategy 2047'.
Nipah virus is a zoonotic disease, primarily transmitted from fruit bats.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI decides the repo rate.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, addresses individual privacy rights.
India's first National Quantum Computing Hub is established in Bengaluru.
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Namami Gange Mission aims for 'Aviral and Nirmal Ganga' (uninterrupted and clean Ganga).
The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) comprises six member states in the Gulf region.
Vande Bharat Sleeper trains are part of Indian Railways' modernization plan.
Gender Budgeting analyzes government budgets from a gender perspective.

GS1.headline: New Archaeological Discovery Sheds Light on Indus Valley Civilization's Trade Networks
GS1.explainer:
• Archaeologists in Gujarat announced the discovery of new artifacts and structural remains, providing fresh insights into the trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The findings include unique pottery, seals, and remnants of a dock-like structure, suggesting extensive maritime trade with West Asia.
• This discovery challenges some existing theories about the geographical extent and economic activities of the Harappan civilization.
• The ongoing excavation aims to uncover more details about the daily life, social structure, and technological advancements of the ancient inhabitants.
• Such discoveries are crucial for reconstructing India's ancient history and understanding its contributions to global trade routes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Ancient History
prelimsPoints: New discovery in Gujarat related to Indus Valley Civilization.
Findings suggest maritime trade with West Asia.
Lothal and Dholavira are other important IVC sites in Gujarat.
mainsPoints: Discuss how recent archaeological discoveries have enhanced our understanding of the urban planning and trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Analyze the significance of maritime trade for the prosperity and cultural exchange of ancient Indian civilizations.
Examine the methodologies employed by archaeologists to reconstruct the socio-economic life of ancient societies.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists meticulously excavating a site, revealing ancient pottery fragments.
GS1.trivia: Lothal, an ancient city of the Indus Valley Civilization in Gujarat, is believed to have had one of the world's earliest known docks.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils 'National Urban Development Strategy 2047' to Drive Sustainable Growth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive strategy aiming for planned and sustainable urbanization across India by 2047.
• Key pillars include smart infrastructure development, green urban spaces, affordable housing, and digital governance.
• The strategy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach involving central, state, and local bodies, along with private sector participation.
• Focus areas include enhancing urban mobility, improving waste management, and ensuring climate-resilient urban planning.
• This initiative is envisioned as a critical component of India's 'Viksit Bharat @2047' roadmap.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
Target year for strategy implementation is 2047.
Aims to integrate various urban development schemes under one umbrella.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges of rapid urbanization in India and how this strategy aims to address them.
Evaluate the potential impact of a 'Viksit Bharat @2047' focused urban strategy on economic growth and social equity.
Analyze the role of technology and public-private partnerships in achieving the goals of sustainable urban development.
GS2.imageDescription: Blueprint of a futuristic smart city with green spaces and efficient transport systems.
GS2.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making sustainable urban planning crucial.

GS2.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Tech, Defence, and Green Energy
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral ties between the two nations.
• Discussions focused on deepening cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, defence industrial collaboration, and renewable energy.
• Both sides emphasized expanding trade relations and enhancing coordination on global issues, including climate change and regional security.
• The dialogue also addressed challenges posed by geopolitical shifts and committed to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• This high-level engagement signifies the continued strengthening of the India-US comprehensive global strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Strategic Dialogue is an annual event.
Key areas of cooperation include defence, technology, and clean energy.
Both nations are committed to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India-US strategic partnership and its significance for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss how cooperation in critical and emerging technologies can bolster India's economic growth and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the role of India and the US in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
GS2.imageDescription: Diplomats from India and the US shaking hands after signing agreements.
GS2.trivia: The US is one of India's largest trading partners and a key source of Foreign Direct Investment (FDI).

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling dismissed petitions challenging the Act on grounds of privacy infringement and potential misuse of state power.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• This landmark judgment provides legal clarity and strengthens India's framework for data governance in the digital age.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data, recognizing the right of individuals to protect their personal data.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of IT in governance.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act 2023 was enacted to protect personal data.
Supreme Court upheld its key provisions.
The Act balances individual privacy with state interests.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the balance between individual privacy and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the significance of the DPDP Act 2023 in shaping India's digital economy and protecting citizen's rights.
Critically evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a diverse and digitally evolving nation.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books in front of the Supreme Court of India emblem.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Inclusive Global AI Governance Framework at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its call for a comprehensive and inclusive global framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance at a United Nations forum.
• Emphasizing ethical considerations, data privacy, and equitable access, India highlighted the need for AI development to benefit all nations.
• The Indian delegation stressed the importance of preventing AI misuse and bridging the digital divide between developed and developing countries.
• This stance aligns with India's domestic efforts to regulate AI while fostering innovation through initiatives like the National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence.
• India's growing technological prowess positions it as a key voice in shaping international norms for responsible AI.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India advocates for an inclusive global AI governance framework.
UN forum is the platform for discussion.
Ethical AI and data privacy are key concerns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in shaping global AI governance norms.
Analyze the ethical dilemmas associated with advanced AI and the need for international cooperation to address them.
Examine how India's domestic AI strategy aligns with its vision for a globally responsible AI ecosystem.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized graphic representing global connectivity and artificial intelligence, with India's flag subtly integrated.
GS2.trivia: India is home to one of the largest pools of AI professionals and researchers globally.

GS2.headline: India-GCC Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stage
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) have reportedly entered their final stages.
• The agreement aims to boost bilateral trade, investment, and economic cooperation between India and the six GCC member states.
• Key areas of discussion include tariff reduction, market access for goods and services, and investment protection.
• A successful FTA would unlock significant economic opportunities for India, particularly in energy, food security, and remittances.
• This move underscores India's 'Look West' policy and its strategic importance to the Gulf region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: GCC comprises six member states: Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE.
FTA aims to boost trade and investment.
India has strong historical ties with the Gulf region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the GCC countries.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India's economy if the FTA is successfully implemented.
Evaluate how India's 'Look West' policy complements its broader foreign policy objectives, particularly in the context of energy security and diaspora engagement.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states arranged side by side, symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The GCC is a political and economic union of Arab states bordering the Persian Gulf.

GS2.headline: Government Releases Gender Budgeting Report 2026, Highlights Progress in Women-Centric Schemes
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance released its annual Gender Budgeting Statement for 2026, detailing allocations and expenditures on programs benefiting women.
• The report highlighted increased outlays for schemes related to women's health, education, skill development, and safety.
• It also assessed the impact of various government initiatives on gender equality and women's empowerment.
• Gender budgeting is a tool to analyze the government budget from a gender perspective to ensure that policies and programs address gender gaps.
• This continuous focus on gender-responsive budgeting is vital for achieving inclusive growth and fulfilling Sustainable Development Goals.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Finance releases Gender Budgeting Statement annually.
It analyzes government budget from a gender perspective.
Aims to address gender gaps and promote women's welfare.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of gender budgeting and its significance in promoting gender equality and women's empowerment in India.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of government schemes designed for women's welfare, as reflected in the Gender Budgeting Report.
Discuss the challenges in implementing gender-responsive budgeting and suggest measures to enhance its impact.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic showing a balance scale with 'Gender Equality' on one side and 'Budget Allocation' on the other.
GS2.trivia: India introduced Gender Budgeting in 2005-06, becoming one of the first countries in the world to adopt this practice.

GS3.headline: Clashes Erupt in Kokrajhar, Assam, Following Youth's Murder; Two Killed
GS3.explainer:
• Violent clashes broke out in Kokrajhar district, Assam, leading to the death of at least two individuals and several injuries.
• The unrest was triggered by the murder of a local youth, escalating into ethnic tensions in some areas.
• Authorities have imposed Section 144 of CrPC and deployed additional security forces to restore order.
• The Chief Minister has appealed for peace and announced a high-level inquiry into the incident.
• This incident highlights the persistent challenges of ethnic harmony and internal security in certain regions of Assam.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
prelimsPoints: Kokrajhar district is located in Assam.
Section 144 of CrPC prohibits assembly of more than four people.
Assam has a history of ethnic and communal tensions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to ethnic clashes in regions like Kokrajhar and suggest long-term solutions.
Discuss the role of state and non-state actors in aggravating internal security challenges in the North-Eastern states.
Examine the effectiveness of administrative measures like Section 144 in managing law and order situations during civil unrest.
GS3.imageDescription: Security personnel patrolling a tense street in Kokrajhar, Assam.
GS3.trivia: Kokrajhar is part of the Bodoland Territorial Region (BTR), an autonomous administrative unit in Assam.

GS3.headline: Nipah Virus Outbreak Reported in West Bengal: Health Alert Issued
GS3.explainer:
• An outbreak of the Nipah virus has been confirmed in West Bengal, with at least two cases reported in Barasat, North 24 Parganas district.
• Health authorities have initiated contact tracing, surveillance measures, and established isolation facilities.
• The state government has issued a high alert, advising public on preventive measures and symptoms.
• Nipah virus is a zoonotic disease transmitted from animals (primarily fruit bats) to humans, or through contaminated food, or directly from person-to-person.
• This outbreak underscores the continuous threat of emerging infectious diseases and the need for robust public health infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Nipah virus is caused by a paramyxovirus.
Fruit bats (Pteropus genus) are natural hosts.
Symptoms include fever, headache, drowsiness, disorientation, and mental confusion.
Kerala has previously experienced Nipah outbreaks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in managing zoonotic disease outbreaks like Nipah in a densely populated country like India.
Evaluate the preparedness of India's public health infrastructure to handle emerging infectious diseases.
Explain the 'One Health' approach and its significance in preventing and controlling zoonotic pathogens.
GS3.imageDescription: A medical professional in PPE conducting tests in a makeshift laboratory.
GS3.trivia: The Nipah virus was first identified in 1998 during an outbreak among pig farmers in Malaysia.

GS3.headline: Indian Air Force Pipistrel Virus Aircraft Crashes in Prayagraj; No Casualties
GS3.explainer:
• An Indian Air Force (IAF) Pipistrel Virus SW 80 aircraft experienced an engine failure and crashed into a pond near Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh.
• Both pilots safely ejected before the crash, and no injuries were reported.
• A Court of Inquiry has been ordered to investigate the exact cause of the engine malfunction.
• The Pipistrel Virus SW 80 is a light trainer aircraft used by the IAF for basic flying training.
• This incident highlights the importance of regular maintenance and safety protocols in military aviation.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Pipistrel Virus SW 80 is an IAF trainer aircraft.
The incident occurred in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh.
No injuries were reported.
GS3.imageDescription: Emergency services personnel inspecting the site of a small aircraft crash.
GS3.trivia: The Pipistrel Virus SW 80 is manufactured by a Slovenian company, Pipistrel, and is known for its fuel efficiency.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Achieves Key Milestones, Boosts Renewable Energy Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission reported significant progress in its initial phase, with several pilot projects achieving operational status.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Key achievements include advancements in electrolyser manufacturing capabilities and development of green hydrogen production facilities.
• Government incentives and partnerships with private sector players are driving innovation and investment in the sector.
• This initiative is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization efforts, and achieving its climate targets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for India to be a global hub for green hydrogen.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy through electrolysis.
It plays a crucial role in India's climate targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and its commitment to climate action.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential of India becoming a global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of a hydrogen molecule with green leaves, symbolizing green energy.
GS3.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030 under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns and Growth Outlook
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• This decision comes amidst persistent inflationary pressures, albeit with signs of moderation, and a stable growth outlook for the Indian economy.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a 'wait and watch' approach, closely monitoring global economic uncertainties and domestic demand.
• The status quo decision impacts lending rates and the overall liquidity in the financial system.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC sets the repo rate.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting is a primary objective of MPC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Analyze the factors influencing the Monetary Policy Committee's decision-making process in the current economic scenario.
Evaluate the impact of a 'status quo' monetary policy on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending and investment.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo with a graph showing economic trends.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members (three from RBI, three appointed by the government).

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase-II Accelerates, Focus on Tributaries and Sustainable River Basin Management
GS3.explainer:
• The second phase of the Namami Gange Mission has shown accelerated progress, with increased focus on cleaning tributaries and adopting a holistic river basin management approach.
• New projects initiated include decentralized wastewater treatment plants, solid waste management in towns along tributaries, and ecological restoration.
• The mission emphasizes community participation and leveraging technology for real-time monitoring of water quality.
• This renewed push aims to achieve the 'Aviral and Nirmal Ganga' (uninterrupted and clean Ganga) vision, extending its impact beyond the main river.
• The success of the mission is crucial for public health, biodiversity, and the cultural heritage associated with the Ganga river system.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Mission aims to clean and rejuvenate the Ganga river.
Phase-II focuses on tributaries and holistic basin management.
Ministry of Jal Shakti is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Assess the challenges faced by large-scale river rejuvenation projects like Namami Gange in India and the strategies adopted to overcome them.
Discuss the socio-economic and ecological benefits of a clean Ganga river system for the states it traverses.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological interventions in ensuring the long-term sustainability of river cleaning initiatives.
GS3.imageDescription: A drone shot of a clean stretch of the Ganga river with new infrastructure visible along its banks.
GS3.trivia: Ganga is the longest river of India, flowing for over 2,500 km, and is considered sacred by millions.

GS3.headline: India Inaugurates Its First National Quantum Computing Hub in Bengaluru
GS3.explainer:
• India officially inaugurated its first National Quantum Computing Hub (NQCH) in Bengaluru, marking a significant leap in advanced technology.
• The hub will serve as a central facility for research, development, and innovation in quantum technologies, including quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
• It aims to foster collaboration between academia, industry, and government to build a robust quantum ecosystem.
• This initiative is part of the National Quantum Mission, which seeks to position India among the leading nations in quantum science and technology.
• Quantum computing holds immense potential for breakthroughs in medicine, materials science, artificial intelligence, and cybersecurity.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: India's first NQCH is in Bengaluru.
Part of the National Quantum Mission.
Focuses on quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of quantum computing for India's national security and economic competitiveness.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in developing a indigenous quantum technology ecosystem in India.
Examine the potential applications of quantum computing across various sectors and its transformative impact.
GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of quantum bits (qubits) and a microchip, symbolizing advanced computing.
GS3.trivia: A 'qubit' (quantum bit) is the basic unit of information in quantum computing, analogous to a bit in classical computing.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Promote Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched a new scheme aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture across vulnerable regions of India.
• The scheme focuses on developing and disseminating stress-tolerant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It integrates traditional knowledge with modern scientific approaches to enhance farmers' adaptability to changing climatic conditions.
• Financial incentives, training programs, and capacity building initiatives are central to the scheme's implementation.
• This move is critical for ensuring food security, increasing farmers' income, and achieving sustainable agricultural development in the face of climate change.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
keyTerms: National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change (NAFCC)
prelimsPoints: New scheme by Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare.
Aims to promote climate-resilient agriculture.
Focus on stress-tolerant crops and efficient water management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by Indian agriculture due to climate change and the need for climate-resilient practices.
Evaluate the potential impact of government schemes promoting climate-smart agriculture on farmers' livelihoods and national food security.
Analyze how traditional agricultural knowledge can be integrated with modern science to build a more resilient farming system.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with modern irrigation techniques and drought-resistant crops.
GS3.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture, with millions of farmers dependent on monsoon rains.

GS3.headline: First Vande Bharat Sleeper Train Prototype Unveiled, Set for Trials
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Railways unveiled the prototype of its first Vande Bharat Sleeper train, designed to offer premium overnight travel experience.
• The new train set features enhanced amenities, including improved berths, modular interiors, and advanced safety systems.
• This development is part of the broader modernization plan for Indian Railways, aiming to replace conventional sleeper coaches on key routes.
• The Vande Bharat Sleeper is expected to undergo extensive trials before commercial rollout, promising faster and more comfortable long-distance journeys.
• This initiative is a significant step towards 'Make in India' in the railway sector and upgrading passenger experience.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: First Vande Bharat Sleeper prototype unveiled.
Aims to replace conventional sleeper coaches.
Part of Indian Railways' modernization plan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of railway modernization, exemplified by Vande Bharat trains, in enhancing India's connectivity and economic growth.
Analyze the 'Make in India' initiative's impact on the indigenous manufacturing capabilities of the Indian railway sector.
Evaluate how improved passenger experience and faster travel can contribute to regional development and tourism.
GS3.imageDescription: A sleek, modern Vande Bharat Sleeper train prototype standing at a railway platform.
GS3.trivia: The first Vande Bharat Express (trainset 18) was flagged off in February 2019.

GS3.headline: Ecologists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
GS3.explainer:
• A team of ecologists announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats of India.
• The discovery underscores the immense ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot.
• This new species, yet to be formally named, was found in a previously unexplored microhabitat, indicating vast unexplored biodiversity.
• The finding calls for intensified conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the region from habitat loss and climate change.
• Such discoveries contribute significantly to our understanding of India's natural heritage and the global biodiversity inventory.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: New amphibian species discovered in Western Ghats.
Western Ghats is a recognized biodiversity hotspot.
Discovery highlights need for conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the implications of discovering new species for conservation strategies and understanding ecosystem health.
Examine the role of scientific research and community involvement in protecting India's rich biodiversity.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a newly discovered amphibian, showcasing its unique features.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Sees Boost with New Initiatives and Digital Push
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism announced new initiatives to further boost India's tourism sector, focusing on sustainable tourism and cultural heritage.
• These include enhanced digital platforms for tourist information, promotion of lesser-known destinations, and improved connectivity.
• The initiatives aim to attract both domestic and international tourists, contributing significantly to economic recovery and employment generation.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing niche tourism segments like eco-tourism, adventure tourism, and medical tourism.
• A robust tourism sector is crucial for showcasing India's soft power and cultural diversity on the global stage.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Tourism leads initiatives.
Focus on digital platforms and lesser-known destinations.
Aims to boost domestic and international tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional impact of a thriving tourism sector on India's economy, employment, and soft power.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's tourism industry and how recent government initiatives aim to address them.
Evaluate the potential of niche tourism segments like eco-tourism and medical tourism in promoting sustainable and inclusive growth.
GS3.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments and natural landscapes.
GS3.trivia: The 'Incredible India' campaign is a major initiative by the Ministry of Tourism to promote India as a tourist destination.
